context: Subfalcine / Cingulate Herniation (most common) =  contralateral shift of midline structures under falx cerebri =  herniation of cingulate gyrus across falx cerebri Risk:     compression of one of anterior cerebral arteries May be associated with:   transtentorial herniation superior frontal gyrus ← compression of ACA / pericallosal artery √  early signs: @    falx √  shift of ipsilateral cingulate gyrus beneath falx √  deviation of anterior falx with widened CSF space at contralateral side N.B.:   posterior falx remains relatively undisplaced due to greater height + rigidity @    cingulate gy
